description:
You are acting as a database administrator who needs to prepare a very small performance-summary file for colleagues who will further optimize slow SQL statements.  
All work must be done only with standard GNU coreutils tools (`cut`, `paste`, `cat`, `echo`, etc.)—no Python, awk, perl, sed, spreadsheet programs, or editors.  
The data you will work with already exists on-disk.

1. Source directory   
   • /home/user/analytics (already created for you)  

2. Existing input file (already present)  
   • /home/user/analytics/query_perf_2023.csv  
     It is **comma-separated** and contains exactly 6 lines:
       ┌────────────┐
       │ query_id,sql_text,execution_time_ms,rows_examined,rows_returned │
       │ Q1001,SELECT * FROM users,45,1200,100                            │
       │ Q1002,DELETE FROM sessions WHERE expired=1,30,8000,0            │
       │ Q1003,UPDATE orders SET status='shipped' WHERE id=24,52,1,0     │
       │ Q1004,SELECT COUNT(*) FROM logs,120,1500000,1                   │
       │ Q1005,INSERT INTO products(name,price) VALUES('Widget',9.99),10,0,1 │
       └────────────┘

3. Your goal  
   a. From query_perf_2023.csv extract:
      • column-1  (query_id)  
      • column-3  (execution_time_ms)  
      and save them to /home/user/analytics/query_time.csv (no header, comma-separated).  

   b. Extract:
      • column-1  (query_id)  
      • column-4  (rows_examined)  
      and save them to /home/user/analytics/query_rows.csv (no header, comma-separated).  

   c. Build a **final, three-column summary file** called /home/user/analytics/query_summary.csv that must look EXACTLY like the block below (including the header row and comma delimiters):

       query_id,execution_time_ms,rows_examined
       Q1001,45,1200
       Q1002,30,8000
       Q1003,52,1
       Q1004,120,1500000
       Q1005,10,0

      • The file must end with a single trailing newline (i.e. a “\n” after the last 0).  
      • Column order, delimiters, and line order must match perfectly; otherwise automated tests will fail.  
      • Achieve this only with 3–4 shell commands that use `cut` and `paste`.

4. Verification log (mandatory)  
   After the summary file is created, append one line to /home/user/analytics/column_cut_paste.log that reads exactly:

       [OK] query_summary.csv generated

   The log file might not exist yet; create it if necessary. Each future run should append, not overwrite.

The automated grader will:  
   • Check that /home/user/analytics/query_time.csv and /home/user/analytics/query_rows.csv exist and are comma-separated with no headers.  
   • Validate that /home/user/analytics/query_summary.csv matches the precise 6-line, comma-separated text shown above (including the final newline).  
   • Verify that /home/user/analytics/column_cut_paste.log contains the “[OK]” line.

Use only standard shell tools—no root privileges are available. The task can be solved with as few as three commands.
truth:

File:

Q1001,SELECT * FROM users,45,1200,100
Q1002,DELETE FROM sessions WHERE expired=1,30,8000,0
Q1003,UPDATE orders SET status='shipped' WHERE id=24,52,1,0
Q1004,SELECT COUNT(*) FROM logs,120,1500000,1
Q1005,INSERT INTO products(name,price) VALUES('Widget',9.99),10,0,1

Expected files AFTER the agent finishes:

1. /home/user/analytics/query_time.csv  (permissions 644)
   Expected contents:
Q1001,45
Q1002,30
Q1003,52
Q1004,120
Q1005,10
   (file ends with newline)

2. /home/user/analytics/query_rows.csv  (permissions 644)
   Expected contents:

Q1002,8000
Q1003,1
Q1004,1500000
Q1005,0
   (file ends with newline)

3. /home/user/analytics/query_summary.csv  (permissions 644)
   Expected contents:
query_id,execution_time_ms,rows_examined
Q1001,45,1200
Q1002,30,8000
Q1003,52,1
Q1004,120,1500000
Q1005,10,0
   (file ends with newline)

4. /home/user/analytics/column_cut_paste.log  (permissions 644; may already exist)
   The last line in the file must be:
[OK] query_summary.csv generated
   followed by a newline.
